reviews.review_title: Worth the Price and Full of Potential
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoy this game. I like how immersive it is, and the graphics are quite decent. It seems to be heading in the right direction, and I'm a fan of the overall concept.
Since it's still a work in progress, I don't expect perfection. That said, for the price, it's definitely worth it, even in its current state. I'm looking forward to seeing how it develops over time.

Nice work! 

Cheers.

Starting cutscene sounds like it was recorded on a laptop mic from 2009, and all textures in the game look like they were AI generated, and there is ZERO explanation of what you need to do. Just get no mans sky.

reviews.review_title: This is a quick and dirty money grab
reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing VR since 2016 when they first came out. I own all versions of the Rift and Quest. I produced the most comprehensive guide to “Mages Tale”. All this to say I can operate a VR game. This game was garbage and over advertised.

Graphics are poor, mission designs are repetitive and poor with little mission information and general documentation is lacking.
You have to repeat the same things again and again and again to figure out what your supposed to do and how

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We understand that as a long-time VR user with extensive experience, your expectations are high, and we appreciate your dedication to the medium.

We’re sorry to hear that the game didn’t meet your expectations. We acknowledge that there have been concerns regarding the graphics and mission design, and we want to assure you that we have been actively working on improvements in both areas. Recent updates have enhanced the visual quality, and we’ve made adjustments to the mission flow and provided clearer guidance for players.

We value all feedback, both positive and negative, as it helps us to continue refining the experience. Our team is committed to delivering a better game for everyone, and we will keep working on future updates to address the points you've mentioned.

Thank you for your time, and we hope to earn back your trust.

reviews.review_description:
I have played Red Matter I and II, Lone Echo I and II, and although it is not at that level, I find it enjoyable to play. Graphics are good, and I like having to figure things out - to a point. So far, I have gotten to collecting the 2nd helix, but I am having a hell of a time escaping the robots. As soon as they get you, your right back to the chamber to start over.

My biggest complaint is how the Oculus buttons are set up for use. 1st - Using the grip button is not, and should not, be how you fire a weapon. 2nd - the B button should not be the one used to change the projectiles. This is clumsy, and seems like it was developed by someone who is not used to the Oculus handhelds. 

Easy fix?
1st - use the grip buttons for jetting up or down, and the trigger for starting the ship, pulling items with the tractor beam, and changing the projectiles. This is the way most other experiences are set up.
2nd - make the B button as the means of grabbing the gun and use the A button for changing the projectiles. Far easier to make a defense against attacking robots.
The way it is currently set up is not intuitive and is completely unnecessary. At least make it so that the user can choose the option to switch button use themselves.

3rd - (spoiler) it seems like option A in normal mode is typically a dead end? I have played a lot of puzzle games (Riven, Myst, Obduction) but these options seem unsolvable. B options are tough (that's OK) but at least they are solvable given a few try's. This may be on purpose, or I am missing something.

When I first loaded this up, I loved it, jet-packing around the space station is great, flying the Kira is great, quite relaxing even. But as soon as mission 2 I realised I had wasted my money. Gameplay that relies on being forced to play the same mission over and over because you haven't been given enough information and the time limit is ridiculously tight is not enjoyable. Stop designing games like this. All it's done is put me in a  bad mood. Never have I gone from fun to frustrated disappointment in such a short space of time. It's a waste of time, and money in the bin.

developer_response.body:
We're sorry to hear about the issue you're experiencing with our game. The problem you've mentioned has already been reported by other players and has now been fixed. A new update with these fixes will be released next week.
We've already made several changes based on your suggestions and will continue to do so.
If you have further concerns or need assistance, please feel free to reach out to our game support.
Thank you for your feedback.

reviews.review_title: Slow burn but worth the effort
reviews.review_description: It took some time to get the hang of controls and getting through missions. Once I got a hang of zero gravity and learned how to access missions, the game really started to pay off. The deep space scene were beautiful, and the dynamic activities kept me going through the game.

Having read some of the developer responses to the reviews, I hoped that they'd had made some changes, but there very little in the way of instructions and you have to repeat the same level over and over again. Its a shame, this game could be good and I was really looking forward to it, but instead its just frustrating and gets boring very very quickly.

developer_response.body:
We apologize for the issues you've encountered. The player base is very diverse, and the feedback we've received ranges from players who find the game too easy to those who find it too difficult. However, we have updated the application, making it extremely easy only for the 'Easy' difficulty level. Please note that you can change the difficulty mode at any time from the menu without affecting your progress or saved data.
We also recently published a walkthrough video for players who might experience difficulties at specific points in the game. You can check it out here: https://youtu.be/KaSUmGDVx3U
Thank you for your feedback, and we will continue working to improve our game.

reviews.review_description:
The game is very engaging, wearing the suit feels great, and piloting the ship is quite fun. At the beginning, there aren’t many instructions, just a mission panel for receiving current assignments and a tutorial panel but after a while, you get the hang of things (tablet is essential). The planetary missions are very well done, and the(few) combat sections are also solid. Overall, there's a lot of variety, different situations to tackle, and a decent storyline.
For me, the game deserves 4 stars, but I’m giving it 5 for one particular feature I’ve always wanted in No Man’s Sky and Starfield, the ability to exit and enter the ship while in space at any time. I also really appreciated the absolute silence in space (since sound doesn't propagate)

It is a great space game. I love the graphics, the movement. You really feel like you are in zero G. The space station is amazing and very detailed. The ship is very responsive and neat to drive.
My problem, if I quit, the game or pause, then I have to start the whole mission over again.
I really like this game. I would compare it to Red Matter and Hubris. Great job developers. Thank you

Awesome! It's not super intuitive at first, there's no real tutorial, just some on screen hints that help explain most things. Once you start getting the hang of it, you realize you're in an open world with lots of different environments and situations, including action, puzzles, and piloting. I really like how you can get in and out of the spaceship anytime, not many games let you do that. A tip for anyone thinking about trying it: the tablet is super important, it tells you what to do after every mission update. Definitely recommended for fans of this genre.

Deep Space Dilemmas is an immersive VR adventure by CODENRAMA, launched in 2024. Set in a futuristic spaceship full of challenges, the game combines problem-solving with action in a way few VR titles actually achieve.  The gameplay mechanics is exploration-driven as well as action oriented. VR controls are essential and intuitive. A combination of Innovative environmental interaction and decision-making make the game unique. Visuals and audio are realistic and performant and music enhances the atmosphere.
The immersion is great as there are moments in which one truly feels “in the game” especially as you can interact with objects and make choices. The Storyline is stimulating  and articulated and  the  setting engaging. Difficulty level can be selected from beginners upward. Also, as there multiple choices, repeat games are encouraged.  The game content absolutely justifies the price. The strengths are the Immersive environments, challenging choices. On the other hand no particular weakness detected. In summary Deep Space Dilemmas offers an exhilarating mix of puzzles and action. It is highly recommended for fans of sci-fi adventures and VR thrill-seekers!
A very engaging game, quite easy but a great ecperience!

I'm very disappointed with this game . Graphics are fantastic but i also  found it to be the most difficult game I've purchased to date . So difficult in fact , that I can't even complete level 1. Aside from the bare mission profile and instructions on how to use your controllers , there's not one single in - game  clue to help you along . After a couple tries , I decided to restart the game from scratch . But then, I could nolonger even find any of those bot sugs or whatever they're called to destroy . Even though one appeared on my first go round . 

When I requested a refund , I received a rejection notification within about 15 minutes. Maybe I played it for more than two hours ?   I don't really know . But it feels so freakin unfair to not be given a refund , for a game that I likely will never be able to complete . 

If the developers read this and change their mind , I'll be very happy and say no harm.done ; this game just wasn't meant for me that's all. But as of now , I can only advise others to be very wary that they may have similar issues with this game app.

developer_response.body: We're very sorry to hear about your disappointment with the game. Unfortunately, we don't have control over the refund system. However, if you would be willing to give the game another chance, feel free to contact our support team, and we will provide you with any useful information to help you. We're confident that once you get a better understanding of the game's basic rules, you'll be able to successfully complete each mission.

reviews.review_description:
Anything to do with Space, I’m into it and this is a reasonably a good game. Be it just floating around in Zero G outside your Space Station, chasing after sugbots, or, wandering around inside your Space Station.

The graphics are really good and the movements of the Astronaut is very precise. A slight touch and off you go.

Mission 1 is interesting, as you have to chase after sugbots outside, hold onto it and try and figure out the four button combination for the self destruction. There is a clue to the correct sequence but that would be a spoiler.

Once you have completed this mission and before you go onto the next. Take a look around the Space Station you are on. Look at the Crew’s Mess, the Captain’s Cabin, Holo Journey - some amazing artwork in these and other places you can visit, if you have the access to gain entry.

Now onto the next mission, in which you have to take your ship to warp speed to get through the wormhole. Good luck with that one…

However, in the initial software, there wasn’t many clues or help available to assist you. So, I went onto the FB page and asked a question. The reply I received was fairly rude as if they were talking down to someone who was brainless and didn’t get it. That made me want to apply for an immediate refund but, as I had played for more than two hours, I lucked out.

New update automatically installed itself but I haven’t gone back to the game since. No longer interested!!

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear that you experienced an issue with our response. After investigating, we can confirm that you were right: in the previous version of the game, the procedure for defeating the Sugbots was not explained correctly, as we intended. We sincerely apologize for this oversight and encourage you to give our updated game another chance. Thank you for your understanding and feedback!

reviews.review_description: This game could be great, but it needs control options added. the no space movement is good. i wish i could adjust the rotation speed. as for flight i wish there was an option to flip the left hand controls to the right hand and vice versa. also an ability to toggle inverted pitch on and off would be great.

Really enjoyed playing this , I loved jetting around in my suit, 

Flying in the ship felt great , my only issue was no snap turn, but I just turned physically so it wasn’t a problem

reviews.review_title: Push Your Critical Thinking To Its Limit
reviews.review_description: Deep Space Dilemma is a game that puts you in some sticky situations throughout the galaxy. You must complete certain tasks around space stations that are all crucial to the mission. Each task is designed to build off each other and really test your patience, quick thinking, and responses. I found this game to run fairly well as its made in unreal engine but there were a few hitches here and there. What I really like about it is the movement system that is very much like Lone Echo. The story is pretty cool as it seems to set you up for why you are in space and cant wait to see more from it. You are able to fly space ships and hit hyper speeds as you use wormholes to move to different areas. Each mission gives you options as you are able to choose different paths in order to complete that objective which i thought was cool. The real heartbeat of this is how much you must think on your feet in order to stay alive and complete your tasks. It really puts the pressure on and makes your brain race like crazy. Pretty fun game for a new released title. I cant wait to see more updates and optimization!

This game has the making of something really good, BUT suffers from a lack of information, meaning you will die multiple times and get stuck as there is stuff you need to do, with no hints or info of how to do it or what to look for (e.g. How does the tractor beam work and how do you operate it???) tried mashing every button on the controller but still no idea, so I'm completely stuck on mission 3. The game's too new to have spawned any support discussions yet and there's nothing online so going to park it for a month or so and come back to see if there are any updates.
Fantastic graphics and atmosphere
In short its a great game and I am a very picky person.
